entries.response:
Here is a simple plant-based sequence diagram for an imaginary example:
```
+---------------+
| #include <PlantUML/sequence_diagram.h>
+---------------+
* The sequence diagram represents a user in the context of a PlantUML file to create, edit, delete or update nodes and edges from a set of nodes and edges. Each node is represented by an `id`, which can be one of:
- ID 1 (a single node with zero value)
- ID 2 (two nodes with two values)
* ID 3 (three nodes with three values)
+---------------+
```
Here's how the diagram looks like:
```
+---------------+
| #include <PlantUML/sequence_diagram.h>
+---------------+
* The sequence diagram represents a user in the context of a PlantUML file to create, edit, delete or update nodes and edges from a set of nodes and edges. Each node is represented by an `id`, which can be one of:
- ID 1 (a single node with zero value)
- ID 2 (two nodes with two values)
* ID 3 (three nodes with three values)
+---------------+
```
Here's how the diagram looks like when a user creates a new node and sets its `id` to the one in the sequence diagram:
```
+---------------+
| #include <PlantUML/sequence_diagram.h>
+---------------+
* The sequence diagram represents a user creating a new node and setting it's `id` to the one in the sequence diagram as in the following code snippet:
```
#include <PlantUML/sequence_diagram.h>
/*
Node creation: 
* ID 1 (a single node with zero value)
+ ID 2 (two nodes with two values)
* ID 3 (three nodes with three values)
* ID 4 (one node with zero value)
+ ID 5 (none other than one node)
* ID 6 (none other than other node)
* ID 7 (none other than other node)
* ID 8 (none other than other node)
* ID 9 (none other than other node)
* ID 10 (none other than other node)
+ ID 11 (none other than other node)
* ID 12 (noneother than other node)
* ID 13 (noneother than other node)
* ID 14 (noneother than other node)
* ID 15 (noneother than other node)
* ID 16 (noneother than other node)
+ ID 17 (noneother than other node)
* ID 18 (noneother than other node)
* ID 19 (noneother than other node)
* ID 20 (noneother than other node)
* ID 21 (noneother than other node)
* ID 22 (noneother than other node)
* ID 23 (noneother than other node)
* ID 24 (noneother than other node)
+ ID 25 (noneother than other node)
* ID 26 (noneelse than other node)
* ID 27 (noneelse than other node)
* ID 28 (noneelse than other node)
* ID 29 (noneelse than other node)
+ ID 30 (noneother than other node)
* ID 31 (noneother than other node)
* ID 32 (noneother than other node)
* ID 33 (noneother than other node)
* ID 34 (noneother than other node)
* ID 35 (noneelse than other node)
* ID 36 (noneelse than other node)
* ID 37 (noneelse than other node)
* ID 38 (noneelse than other node)
* ID 39 (noneother than other node)
* ID 40 (noneelse than other node)
* ID 41 (noneelse than other node)
+ ID 42 (noneelse than other node)
```
